message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  We modelled historical, country-specific routine immunisation trends using publicly available vaccination coverage data for diphtheria, tetanus and pertussis-containing vaccine first-dose (DTP1) and third-dose (DTP3) from 2000 to 2019. We evaluate changes in coverage in 2020 by comparing model predictions to WUENIC-reported coverage. We report a 2.9% (95%
                  <jats:sub>CI</jats:sub>
                  : [2.2%; 3.6%]) global decline in DTP3 coverage, and important increases in missed immunisations in some countries with middle-income countries, and the Americas, being most affected.
                </jats:p>
title: Worldwide routine immunisation coverage regressed during the first year of the COVID-19 pandemic

reference.article-title: Impact of COVID-19 pandemic on mobility in ten countries and associated perceived risk for all transport modes
